Zone of the biggest frauds ever per-
petrated ON this country resulted from
the passage of the forest reserve bill,
said ex-Congressman w. c. Jones this
morning.


Mr.. Jones assertion was in connec-
ton with his views of the timber land
frauds that are now bringing scandal
to land offices In Idaho, Oregon and
many other western states. Continu-
ing, Mr.. Jones explained:


"The special session of congress that
convened In 1897 was the worst trick
ed body Of lawmakers that ever met
IN NEUTRALITY-SUSPICIOUS. the great ma
jOrity of the members thought they
were called to consider the tariff Ques-
ton, whereas in the long list Of bust
ness transacted was buried the section
that robbed the government out of mil
lions of acres of its most valuable tim
Der land.


"The section to which I refer, sec
ton SO, in q list Of nearly 300 minor
sections, provided for the timber re-
serves; and provided that versions
holding land in the desired belt should
be reimbursed with land script. This
